content: We can reframe curiosity-driven autonomous agents to assign intrinsic reward not just to novel unexplored state spaces, but to failed trajectories that the agent’s own self-supervised world model incorrectly predicted. This creates a 'failure curiosity' intrinsic signal that directs active exploration toward high-uncertainty failure modes the agent has not yet mastered, rather than just random unvisited novel states.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
